headline: Jabil (JBL) Valuation Check After Earnings Beat And Higher Full Year Guidance
summary: Jabil (JBL) just posted fiscal second quarter results that came in ahead of analyst expectations and raised its full year outlook, with management pointing to demand across cloud, data center, automotive, and renewable markets. See our latest analysis for Jabil. The latest earnings beat and guidance upgrade have come alongside strong momentum, with a 90 day share price return of 21.29% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 93.75%, pointing to rising enthusiasm around Jabil's role in AI...

headline: Micron Earnings Face High Expectations as Its Stock Keeps Rising
summary: Micron shares have soared more than 60% to start the year, the sixth-best performance in the S&P 500 Index, after jumping 239% in 2025.  The biggest US maker of memory chips is benefiting from heavy spending on artificial intelligence computing that’s driving demand for memory and storage components, as are high-flying peers Sandisk Corp. and Western Digital Corp.

headline: Electronics firm Jabil raises annual forecasts on data center demand
summary: Electronic component maker Jabil raised its full-year profit and revenue forecasts on Wednesday, ‌betting on strong demand for infrastructure services from ‌AI data centers. Shares of the St. Petersburg, Florida-based company were up 1.1% ​in premarket trading, after it surpassed Wall Street expectations for second-quarter results. A surge in spending for data-center infrastructure, driven by robust demand for computing power to support AI ‌technologies, has benefited ⁠companies such as Jabil.

headline: Jabil’s (NYSE:JBL) Q1 CY2026: Strong Sales
summary: Electronics manufacturing services provider Jabil (NYSE:JBL) reported revenue ahead of Wall Street’s expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 23.1% year on year to $8.28 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($8.5 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 6% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.69 per share was 7.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

raw.headline: Jabil’s (NYSE:JBL) Q1 CY2026: Strong Sales

On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($8.5 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 6% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.69 per share was 7.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Jabil: Fiscal Q2 Earnings Snapshot
